title: Did Anno actually DIRECT Evangelion
wikitext:
[]: Did Anno actually DIRECT Evangelion?             Quote Post by thedarkorb » Tue Sep 30, 2008 3:48 pm So im a little confused as to whether or not Anno directed the actual episodes. I always was marveled by how all the shots and angles where arranged and assumed they where done by Anno himself... http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_Neon_Genesis_Evangelion_episodes ^ Wiki lists different directors for each of the episodes, with Anno only credited for writing. Did Anno merely direct the actors voices?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Sep 30, 2008 3:56 pm http://evageeks.org/FGP/Hideaki_Anno Our wiki states he's the director.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Sep 30, 2008 4:16 pm Anno is the "kantoku" （監督）, which is equivalent to "director" in film terms, and refers to the supervisory role. The individual episode "directors" are credited with　"enshutsu" (演出), which Eijirou gives as "producer" and also "director" (with a different nuance, probably). So Anno's the head honcho, but there are various minions giving more direct attention to the individual episodes.

[]:             Quote Post by AyrYntake » Tue Sep 30, 2008 4:20 pm I'll second that. Basically, he takes credit for all of the storyline and most of the style (designating camera angles, sounds, shots, panning and all that).

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Sep 30, 2008 4:28 pm The storyline was hardly created in a vacuum, so "all" is perhaps a bit strong.

[]:             Quote Post by AyrYntake » Tue Sep 30, 2008 4:31 pm Point taken. Still, you have to admit it was a far superior take on some pretty old ideas...so a lot of credit to Anno for that. [/lame defense]

[]:             Quote Post by thedarkorb » Tue Sep 30, 2008 4:47 pm Looks like that clears that up then. Good info.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Sep 30, 2008 4:50 pm @orb: Glad to help. @Ayn: Just to avoid any vaguery, by "not in a vacuum" I specifically meant "there were other people chipping in to bring about the NGE we know". The people at EML make a big deal about Sadamoto's contributions (probably because they dislike criticism of the manga) , and, while I'm grotesquely lacking expertise on NGE's pre-production, I would guess that people like Tsurumaki, Masayuki, Yamashita, and various others had an impact, as well. Stinks that translated information is all over the place and most information isn't translated in the first place.

[]:             Quote Post by Chrad » Tue Oct 07, 2008 6:50 am It's also worth noting that Anno was sole director of 26'.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Tue Oct 07, 2008 11:07 am "Head Director" (or whatever) of a Japanese anime seems to be more like "Overseer" or "Executive Producer" than anything else.

[]:             Quote Post by Chrad » Wed Oct 08, 2008 9:20 am I'd say it's a bit more than that though, given that you can see in Eva much of Anno's signature directorial style (based on his other works, like Shiki-Jitsu and Kare Kano) and little of the style of say, Tsurumaki.
summary: []: Did Anno actually DIRECT Evangelion?             Quote Post by thedarkorb » Tue Sep 30, 2008 3:48 pm So im a little confused as to whether or not Anno directed the actual episodes.
